commit.message:
fix: increase FunctionGemma MAX_TOKENS 256→1024 to match ekv1024 model (#207)

The model file is the ekv1024 variant (1024-token KV cache) but the session
was created with maxNumTokens=256, causing every inference call to fail with
'Input token ids are too long: 319 >= 256'. System prompt + skill declarations
alone exceed 256 tokens before the user message is even appended.

Co-authored-by: NickMonrad <nick@lab3.com.au>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add ESLint with React Compiler and React Hooks rules (#27)

Adds ESLint to enforce Rules of React via `eslint-plugin-react-compiler`
and `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`, with a CI job that fails on any
violation.

### Setup
- ESLint 9 flat config with `typescript-eslint` recommended, React Hooks
recommended, and React Compiler (`error` severity)
- `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` configured to allow `_`-prefixed
identifiers

### CI
- New **Lint** job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `pnpm run lint`

### Fixes
- Prefixed unused `db` parameter in `src/db/migrations.ts` with `_` to
satisfy `no-unused-vars`

```js
// eslint.config.js
export default tseslint.config(
  js.configs.recommended,
  ...tseslint.configs.recommended,
  {
    plugins: { "react-hooks": reactHooks, "react-compiler": reactCompiler },
    rules: {
      ...reactHooks.configs.recommended.rules,
      "react-compiler/react-compiler": "error",
    },
  },
);
```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: valscion <482561+valscion@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: shubhodeep1 <11442166+shubhodeep1@users.noreply.github.com>

Split UI vs backend card removal; animate only backend-initiated deletes

UI dismiss (✕ button, action button):
- Instant DOM removal, no animation, no indicator flash
- Fires DELETE / action API after element is already gone

Backend-initiated removal (SSE reason:'delete'):
- handleSSEUpdate routes delete events to animateBackendRemoval()
- Phase 1 (0.8 s): indicator breathes red + card fades to red
- Phase 2 (0.2 s): card height collapses so remaining cards slide up smoothly
- Only then is the element removed — user sees the heads-up before any
  layout change
- If card is already gone (we dismissed it locally), the SSE event is
  silently ignored (no double-animation)

SSE reason:'new'/'action' still calls fetchNotifications() as before

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implemented pack/default-aware primary variable-template instantiation (#1241)

* Implemented pack/default-aware primary variable-template instantiation

Primary variable templates now canonicalize arguments like class templates do, so variadic packs and omitted default parameters are bound before substitution, and their initializers go through the generic pack-aware template substitutor instead of the old fixed-arity map path.

* Fix variable template review follow-ups

Address the substantive PR #1241 review feedback around defaulted type materialization, empty pack handling, and inner template argument forwarding. Refresh the representative std-header findings to match the current compiler behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix stale browser cache / "module factory not available" caused by service worker (#329)

- Fix stale browser cache caused by service worker
- pages/_app.tsx: unregister any lingering SWs in non-production to
  prevent stale cached responses from interfering with HMR in dev mode
- next.config.js: add Cache-Control: no-cache headers for sw.js so
  browsers always fetch the latest SW script on each navigation
- public/sw.js: skip intercepting /_next/ paths entirely (Next.js
  content-addresses these; let the browser's HTTP cache handle them);
  change remaining asset strategy from cache-first to
  stale-while-revalidate; bump cache name to bfree-v2 to evict old cache

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Consistent truncation with single-char ellipsis across all tables

- Switch truncate() from '...' (3 chars) to '…' (1 char), reclaiming
  2 chars of visible content per truncated cell
- renderTable now truncates cells at w-1 instead of w, guaranteeing
  1 trailing space on every truncated cell so there is always a visible
  gap before the next column (combined with lipgloss separator = 2 spaces)
- truncateID call sites updated to w-1=14 to stay within budget
- Remove manual truncate(title, 13) hack in search.go; renderTable handles it
- extractSnippet updated to use '…' prefix/suffix for consistency
- Update TestTruncate expectations to reflect new single-char ellipsis

commit.message:
feat: Karpathy's Knowledge Wiki pattern (#250-255) — 6 features

Implements the persistent, compounding wiki pattern from
gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f

WikiEngine (#250):
- .caduceus/wiki/ directory with CRUD, [[link]] parsing
- git-tracked markdown files that persist across sessions

WikiIndex (#251):
- Auto-maintained index.md with categories + summaries
- Orphan page detection, query search

WikiLog (#252):
- Append-only log.md: '## [2026-04-12] ingest | Title'
- Stats aggregation (ingests, queries, lints)

WikiIngestor (#253):
- Extract entities + key claims from raw sources
- Generate summary/entity pages with cross-references
- slugify('My Title') → 'my-title'

WikiLinter (#254):
- Health checks: orphans, broken links, empty, stale pages

WikiQueryEngine (#255):
- TF-relevance scoring, context gathering, citation extraction

Tests: 986 → 1015 (29 new tests)
Total features: 255 (253 ✅ + 2 💡)

commit.message:
Handle Android semantic notification actions

Adds full rendering support for Android's NotificationCompat semantic
action codes (0-10). renderActions() now:

- semanticAction 1 / type 'reply': text input + ↩ Send button
  (input spans full card width; Enter key submits)
- semanticAction 8/9 (👍 👎): emoji-only button
- semanticAction 2-7, 10: icon + action title (e.g. '✓ Mark read')
- Destructive/passive actions (delete 4, mute 6, thumbs down 9)
  rendered in neutral grey instead of green
- No-semantic-action fallback still uses action.type/'reply' check
  and action.title for the button label
- Multiple actions render in a wrapping flex row; reply inputs always
  occupy a full-width row

No backend changes required — actions are stored as-is in the JSON
data column.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: self-ping keep-alive — server pings own health endpoint every 10 min

Root cause: GitHub Actions cron is unreliable for Render free-tier keep-alive.
Crons can be delayed 10-30+ min by GitHub queue pressure, missing the 15-min
Render idle window even with two overlapping schedules.

Fix: server pings its own RENDER_EXTERNAL_URL/api/v1/health every 10 minutes
from inside the Node.js process using built-in https module.
- Render's load balancer sees it as a real incoming HTTP request
- Resets the 15-min idle timer from within the service itself
- No external dependency, no GitHub Actions reliability issues
- First ping fires 5 min after startup (server fully warm first)
- Only active in production with RENDER_EXTERNAL_URL set
- GitHub Actions uptime monitor kept as secondary fallback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: "auto-selected" model placeholder causes 400 model_not_supported; add interactive model selection to config login (#103)

* Initial plan

* Fix: treat \"auto-selected\" model placeholder as unset for GitHub Copilot

When users ran `oxo-call config login` with an older version, the string
"auto-selected" was written into config.toml as the model. Because it is
non-empty, effective_model() returned it verbatim and the GitHub Copilot
API rejected it with 400 model_not_supported.

- effective_model(): skip the stored value when it equals "auto-selected"
  and fall through to the provider default (gpt-4o)
- effective_source("llm.model"): report "provider default" instead of
  "stored config" when the stored model is "auto-selected"
- config login flow: replace the "auto-selected" placeholder (not just
  None) with DEFAULT_COPILOT_LOGIN_MODEL on re-login, self-healing the
  bad stored value

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Traitome/oxo-call/sessions/532e7963-6707-430b-be96-a53a7942477d

Co-authored-by: ShixiangWang <25057508+ShixiangWang@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: interactive model selection in config login for GitHub Copilot

After successful OAuth authorization, show a numbered menu of supported
GitHub Copilot models so the user can choose rather than silently picking
a default.

- COPILOT_MODELS list: gpt-4.1-mini, gpt-4.1, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5,
  claude-3.5-sonnet, claude-sonnet-4, o3-mini
- Free-tier models marked with ⭐ [free tier] and a tip reminding users
  to prefer them
- Default when pressing Enter: gpt-4.1-mini (lightweight, free)
- Non-interactive stdin falls back to default silently
- Removed DEFAULT_COPILOT_LOGIN_MODEL constant (replaced by COPILOT_MODELS[0])
- Update effective_model() github-copilot default to gpt-4.1-mini
- Update docs to reflect new default and interactive model selection step

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Traitome/oxo-call/sessions/df6d95fa-84ba-4407-9f00-8cea30f7295c

Co-authored-by: ShixiangWang <25057508+ShixiangWang@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(tui): overlay system for slash commands + fix text contrast

- Add overlay.rs: SelectionList (interactive picker) + InfoPanel (scrollable)

- Route /status, /cost → styled status info overlay
- Route /help → scrollable info overlay (long output won't scroll away)

- Overlay renders on top of message area, captures all input until Esc
- Fix text-too-white: input text now uses explicit Color::White

- All 373 CLI tests pass

Co-authored-by: Jayson-Furr <53241637+Jayson-Furr@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: skip E2B on FLAGSHIP, disable broken SM8550 embedding variant (#205)

* fix: skip E2B on FLAGSHIP, disable broken SM8550 embedding variant

- ModelDownloadManager.init now skips GEMMA_4_E2B on FLAGSHIP tier — E4B
  is auto-queued via the tier-optional loop instead, preventing both from
  downloading simultaneously with E2B finishing first
- areRequiredModelsDownloaded() accepts E4B OR E2B on FLAGSHIP so the chat
  screen doesn't show ModelsNotReady after E4B downloads
- EMBEDDING_GEMMA_300M_SM8550.preferredForTier = null (was FLAGSHIP) — the
  SM8550 .tflite variant fails with "Unsupported file format" on device
- OnboardingViewModel.preferredEmbeddingModel always uses the generic
  EMBEDDING_GEMMA_300M until the SM8550 variant is fixed upstream

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct FLAGSHIP download progress tracking, remove broken SM8550 embedding candidate

- ModelDownloadManager exposes deviceTier property
- ChatViewModel builds displayModels list from FLAGSHIP-aware logic (E4B
  included, E2B excluded) so anyDownloading and progress are correct during
  E4B download on S23 Ultra
- LiteRtEmbeddingEngine.modelFileCandidates() no longer tries SM8550 file
- One-shot cleanup deletes SM8550 file on cold start if present on disk

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: move SM8550 cleanup to Dispatchers.IO — no disk I/O on main thread

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: NickMonrad <nick@lab3.com.au>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Reduce TITLE column width and add spacing gap in pm search

Reduce TITLE from 20 to 15 chars, truncate content at 13 so padRight
always leaves ≥2 trailing spaces. Combined with lipgloss's 1-space
separator this gives a 3-char visual gap between truncated titles and
MATCH content, preventing the adjacent ellipses from blurring together.

commit.message:
CRPS: Contrastive Reasoning Path Synthesis

Complete implementation of the CRPS framework:
- MCTS exploration with batch search (Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct explorer)
- Distribution-aware contrastive pair sampling (hard/soft negatives)
- Dual-granularity contrastive analysis (gpt-5-mini analyst)
- Pattern-informed path synthesis with verification
- SFT training pipeline (DeepSpeed ZeRO-3)
- vLLM-based evaluation with SymPy answer verification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: initial SDK monorepo with Python + TypeScript packages

- Python SDK (opendata-sdk): httpx + pydantic, sync/async clients, 50 tests
- TypeScript SDK (@opendata/sdk): zero deps, native fetch, 49 tests
- CI/CD: test matrix (Python 3.9-3.12 + Node 20), PyPI OIDC + npm provenance publish
- Full API coverage: products, groups, search, export, auto-pagination, typed errors
- Cursor-based pagination with search_all/searchAll iterators
- Exponential backoff retry on 429/5xx with jitter
- Quota tracking from response headers

commit.message:
feat: reconnect to stored outbound peers on startup (#313)

* Initial plan

* feat: connect to stored outbound peers on startup

- Add get_outbound_peers(limit) to storage/core.rs to query peers with
  non-null multiaddr ordered by last_seen DESC
- Only persist multiaddr for outbound (Dialer) connections in
  event_processor.rs; inbound Listener connections store None
- After swarm.listen_on in main.rs, dial up to 10 stored outbound peers
  at startup so previously-connected peers reconnect automatically
- Add three unit tests for get_outbound_peers
- Update CHANGELOG.md under Unreleased

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bhagdave/p2p-play/sessions/704a0da3-7b8b-4f4e-9e7a-948707ec26b1

Co-authored-by: bhagdave <3230037+bhagdave@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct spelling of multiaddrs in test comments

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bhagdave/p2p-play/sessions/704a0da3-7b8b-4f4e-9e7a-948707ec26b1

Co-authored-by: bhagdave <3230037+bhagdave@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address reviewer feedback on get_outbound_peers and startup logging

- Use i64::try_from(limit) instead of limit as i64 in get_outbound_peers
- Propagate row-mapping errors via collect::<Result<Vec<_>,_>>() instead
  of silently dropping them with filter_map
- Add test_get_outbound_peers_orders_by_last_seen_desc to verify ORDER BY
  last_seen DESC behavior with deterministic timestamps
- Surface multiaddr parse errors and DB load failures in the TUI via
  app.add_to_log in addition to the error! log call

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bhagdave/p2p-play/sessions/fedd686c-c765-477d-afdf-2260a603eed7

commit.message:
fix: spawn orientation from SpawnPoint rotation + RL reward/epsilon overhaul

Spawn orientation:
- getDefaultSpawnPos now returns rotX/Y/Z/W from the SpawnPoint object
- spawnCurrentVehicleType uses spawn point rotation (not current vehicle rotation)
- Extracted spawnObjToResult() helper to avoid duplicate pos+rot extraction

RL learning fixes:
- CoverageMap now tracks ep_visited (per-episode) separately from total (global)
- Reward structure:
    +10  brand-new cell (global first visit)
    + 1  first visit this episode on known terrain -> follow learned routes
    -0.5 loop within same episode
  This ensures the route to the wall and beyond stays positively rewarded
- EPSILON_DECAY 0.993 -> 0.95: at ep50 epsilon is now ~0.07 not ~0.70
- STUCK_PATIENCE 25 -> 50: less false stuck detections
- Map display: shows ep_visited (solid) vs global-only (░) vs unknown (.)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(conductor): restrict file permissions in Docker image

commit.message:
fix(nav): make Progress and Analytics screens accessible from Profile (#520)

Progress and Analytics screens existed but had no navigation entry point.
Users couldn't access these key features from anywhere in the app UI.

Changes:
- Add "Progress & Stats" section to ProfileScreen with two items:
  - "Progress" -> navigates to progress route (charts, PRs, plateau alerts)
  - "Analytics" -> navigates to analytics route (volume, consistency, insights)
- Wire navigation callbacks in ProfileRoute and GymBroNavGraph
- Add bilingual strings (EN + ES) for labels and subtitles
- Use ShowChart and BarChart icons with AccentAmberStart color
- Position prominently after AI Coach button (key feature visibility)

Progress screen links to Analytics, which now has two entry points:
- From Profile -> Progress -> Analytics (existing)
- From Profile -> Analytics (new direct access)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
